headline: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
detail: Lower-than-expected CPI is positive for growth and tech stocks, as it reduces pressure on the Fed for aggressive rate hikes. This could support your tech-heavy portfolio.
rec: No action yet, but continued easing inflation would be a tailwind for your holdings like QQQ and SMH.

headline: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
detail: This indicates the Fed remains vigilant against inflation, even with easing CPI. Hawkish rhetoric could temper market enthusiasm for growth stocks if it suggests rates will stay higher for longer.
rec: No action, but keep an eye on Fed communications for any shift in stance that could impact your leveraged positions.

headline: Jim Cramer says don't bite on Apple sell call; buy his new chip favorite
detail: This reinforces positive sentiment around the chip sector, a significant part of your portfolio (SOXL, SMH). While he doesn't name a specific chip favorite, it validates your concentration.
rec: No specific action based on this alone, but it supports the chip thesis. The scanner also shows a strong long setup for NVDA (entry 205.12-204.45, stop 203).
